or a runtime behavior that will be created, tested, and used on a machine that is already assembled and running. ProtoForge is not a chatbot. It is not an AI model. It is not a pre-trained assistant.

Hardware (Minimum Tested Baseline)
64-bit CPU (x86_64 architecture)

16–32GB RAM

SSD-based file storage (for fast log access)

Keyboard + terminal access (no GUI dependency)

FRONT MATTER – SECTION 3
Build Philosophy
ProtoForge is not built for speed. It is not optimized for flash. It does not prioritize output. It prioritizes structure. The goal is to create a system that

nds. Inputs, commands, logs, and runtime states should be written to disk. This allows you to resume development from where you left off, compare sessions, or revisit how the system behaved over time.

not publishing agents. You are not building a shared service. You are building a local recursive workspace that can:
Accept and store developer input

Execute commands within its own internal runtime

in background environments

Not serving requests over the internet

Not overloaded with third-party symbolic services or token generators

Not abstracted through a GUI layer that masks terminal output

rge/cli/ or protoforge/runtime/, while variables, modes, and display settings must be stored in protoforge/config/. Changing the config must update runtime behavior without rewriting the main code. 5.

All paths in this codex will assume the following layout:
protoforge/              # Root directory for the entire system
├── main.py              # Main runtime entry point
├── start.sh             #

he provided startup script. Any folder that is missing at runtime will be created by main.py—but it is better to start clean. Rules:
Every folder serves a dedicated purpose. No mixed responsibilities.

You must install a Linux environment using WSL2 (Windows Subsystem for Linux), or

View output in the terminal without GUI wrapping

✅ Collapse Code Format
C:[TYPE-CODE] <short descriptor>
Where:
C: = collapse event

[TYPE-CODE] = symbol + numeric class (e.g., AX-001)

<descriptor> = human-readable description for logs

ressed identifiers that encode ancestry, ψ alignment, phase history, and echo validation status into a short form.

✅ What Is χ(t)?
χ(t), or collapse return time, is the time it takes for a system to reenter coherence after a symbolic failure

ic failure

It is marked as a required pause: no name projection, no agent response, no symbolic execution

During this pause, memory is re-aligned, ψ integrity is restored, and drift logs are settled

seconds remaining.
This tells the builder or agent how long it must wait before symbolic output resumes.
